title: Cedarwood beard balm
subtitle: A conditioning anhydrous balm for beard styling and moisturising with cedarwood and vetiver
excerpt: Beard balm sits between a beard oil and a beard butter in terms of hold. The beeswax at 15% gives enough structure to tame flyaways and provide a light style hold without the stiffness of a wax. The formula is anhydrous, requires no preservative, and has a 12-month shelf life.
sourceNotes: Beard balm formulation based on natural cosmetics formulation references and standard anhydrous balm structure.

recipe.yieldDescription: 60 g beard balm (fills a 60 ml tin)
recipe.batchNotes: Scale proportionally. Makes multiple tins at once for gifting.
recipe.temperatureNote: Melt beeswax and shea butter gently. Add jojoba and essential oils once off the heat and below 40 degrees Celsius.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: 12 months in a cool place. Discard if the balm smells rancid.

recipeTools.notes: Accurate to 0.1 g for essential oils

glossaryTerms.definition: Containing no water. Anhydrous products do not support microbial growth and require no preservative.
Used at 10 to 20% in balms to provide structure and light hold. A higher beeswax percentage gives firmer hold; lower percentages give a softer, more conditioning product.
Technically a liquid wax rather than an oil. Exceptionally stable, with a shelf life of several years. It closely resembles the skin and hair's natural sebum and absorbs without greasiness.

A combination of two or more essential oils chosen to create a fragrance. Cedarwood provides a warm, woody base note; sandalwood or vetiver adds depth. The total essential oil content is kept at 3 to 5% for a leave-on product applied to the face.

content.text: at 15% for light hold and structure. Shea butter at 35% provides conditioning.
at 45% makes up the bulk of the formula, absorbing without greasiness. The
of cedarwood and vetiver gives a warm, outdoorsy scent. Apply a small amount to damp beard after washing and work through with fingertips. Shelf life is 12 months.

Melt the beeswax and shea butter together in a small heatproof bowl over a double boiler, stirring until fully liquid. Remove from heat. Add the jojoba oil and stir to combine. Allow to cool to below 40 degrees Celsius, then add both essential oils and stir well. Pour into the tin before the mixture begins to set. Leave to solidify at room temperature for 1 hour.

Store in the tin at room temperature. In very hot weather the balm may soften; store in a cool drawer if this is a problem. Shelf life is 12 months. Discard if the balm smells rancid or the texture separates.
